title: Onagra rubrinervis
taxonomy: Onagra rubrinervis is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, Onagraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Onagra rubrinervis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Myrtales, Family: Onagraceae, Genus: Onagra, Species: O. rubrinervis. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its placement within the plant kingdom. The specific classification of Onagra rubrinervis is still being studied and refined by botanists.

intro: Onagra rubrinervis, also known as Mexican evening primrose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Onagra rubrinervis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Mexican evening primrose include teas, infusions, and capsules, often used to support sleep, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
